element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Basic mechanic available from start; max distance equals screen height
Powerful screen-clearing tool; stress relief mechanic; restocked via 'B' items from ground enemies
Spawned by destroying colored enemy plane waves; increases firepower
Spawned by ground enemies; increases bomb capacity; multiplied by 3x at runway landings
Spawned by colored enemy planes; incentivizes tactical destruction patterns
Death respawns at last reached checkpoint; firepower resets on hit
Halts scrolling; multiplies bombs by 3000 points bonus; marks progression
Applied in loops 2+; requires knowledge-based dodging patterns
Tanks, artillery, battleships; primary sources of 'B' bomb items
Colored waves; primary sources of 'S' power-ups and point bonuses

typical_relief_triggers: Firing bomb to clear screen and damage clustered enemies
Destroying colored enemy plane waves to spawn power-ups
typical_tension_triggers: Narrow dodge windows between enemy projectiles
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Relentless upward pressure from scrolling; wave-based enemy clusters create tension peaks; bomb deployment provides rhythmic relief; difficulty escalates through speed increases and loop restarts

difficulty_curve.early_game: Level 1 designed as introduction for beginning players; basic enemy pacing and slower bullets establish shot/bomb mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Levels 2-4 gradually increase enemy complexity; colored plane waves demand tactical targeting for power-ups
difficulty_curve.late_game: Level 5 presents final military gauntlet; loop restarts increase all difficulty with accelerated bullet speeds
notable_spikes: Loop 2+ onset: bullet speed increases markedly
Level 5: maximum enemy density and coordination
Firepower reset penalty: loss of accumulated power-ups on single hit creates risk/reward tension

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level sequences colored enemy plane waves interspersed with ground vehicle formations; waves spawn item cascades (power-ups, bombs, points) tied to destruction order
Between-level runways function as safe zones where scrolling halts; grants bomb multiplication bonus (3000 pts × bomb count); resets player positioning for next stage
Game restarts after level 5 with same 5-level structure but increased bullet speeds and enemy accuracy; first stage bullet speed returns to normal after overflow condition

sources_used: wikipedia:Flying Shark (article covers arcade original and all ports including NES 1989)
